title: Test Automation with AI (Claude) Learning Path
description: From manual testing to AI-powered automation. Learn Playwright, API testing, CI/CD, and how to use Claude as your testing co-pilot at every step.

phases.title: Phase 1: Testing Foundations
phases.description: Understand the testing pyramid, types of tests, and why automation matters. This is the foundation everything else builds on.

topics.title: The Testing Pyramid
topics.description: Unit tests (fast, many) → Integration tests (medium) → E2E tests (slow, few). Why this shape matters for speed and reliability.

topics.title: Types of Testing
topics.description: Unit, integration, E2E, API, visual regression, performance, security, accessibility. Know when to use each.

topics.title: Write Your First Manual Test Cases
topics.description: Before automating, learn to think like a tester. Write test cases for a login form.
topics.claudePrompt: Write comprehensive test cases for a login form that has email and password fields, a 'Remember Me' checkbox, a 'Forgot Password' link, and a 'Sign In' button. Include happy path, negative tests, edge cases, security tests, and accessibility tests. Format as a table with: ID, Scenario, Steps, Expected Result, Priority (P0/P1/P2).
steps: Pick a feature you use daily (login, search, checkout)
Write 10 test cases covering happy path and edge cases
Ask Claude to review your test cases and suggest ones you missed
Compare your list vs Claude's — what did you miss? What patterns emerge?

topics.title: Test Case Design Techniques
topics.description: Equivalence partitioning, boundary value analysis, decision tables, state transition. The science behind good test cases.
topics.claudePrompt: Explain these test case design techniques with examples for an insurance claims form (claim type: STD/LTD, amount: $0-$50,000, date: within 90 days): 1) Equivalence Partitioning, 2) Boundary Value Analysis, 3) Decision Table Testing, 4) State Transition Testing. Show me the actual test cases each technique would generate.

topics.title: Understanding Test Coverage
topics.description: Line coverage, branch coverage, path coverage. What the numbers mean and what 'good enough' looks like.
topics.claudePrompt: Explain the difference between line coverage, branch coverage, and path coverage with a code example. If I have 80% line coverage but only 50% branch coverage, what does that mean? What's a realistic coverage target for: (a) critical business logic, (b) UI components, (c) utility functions?

phases.checkpoint: You can write comprehensive test cases, understand the testing pyramid, and know which test types apply to different scenarios.

phases.title: Phase 2: JavaScript Testing with Jest
phases.description: Start automating with unit tests. Jest is the most popular JS testing framework and the foundation for everything that follows.

topics.title: Jest Setup & First Test
topics.description: Install Jest, write your first test, run it, see green.
steps: Create a new project: npm init -y && npm install --save-dev jest
Create sum.js: module.exports = (a, b) => a + b
Create sum.test.js: test('adds 1 + 2', () => expect(sum(1, 2)).toBe(3))
Run: npx jest
See the green checkmark — you're a tester now

topics.title: Jest Matchers & Assertions
topics.description: toBe, toEqual, toContain, toThrow, toMatch, toHaveProperty — the vocabulary of testing.
topics.claudePrompt: Show me every commonly used Jest matcher with a practical example for each. Group them by: equality, truthiness, numbers, strings, arrays/objects, exceptions, and async. Use a claims processing system as the example domain.

topics.title: AI-Generated Unit Tests
topics.description: Use Claude to generate tests from your code, then learn from what it produces.
topics.claudePrompt:
Write comprehensive unit tests for this function using Jest:

[PASTE YOUR FUNCTION HERE]

Requirements:
- Cover all branches and conditions
- Include happy path, edge cases, boundary values, and error scenarios
- Use descriptive test names that read like specifications
- Group tests logically with describe blocks
- Aim for >90% branch coverage
steps: Write a utility function (validation, calculation, transformation)
Try writing tests yourself first — aim for 5-8 test cases
Ask Claude to generate tests for the same function
Compare: what did Claude catch that you missed?
Learn the patterns Claude uses — describe blocks, edge cases, naming conventions

topics.title: Mocking & Test Doubles
topics.description: Mocks, spies, stubs — how to isolate the code you're testing from its dependencies (APIs, databases, timers).
topics.claudePrompt:
Explain Jest mocking with practical examples:
1. jest.fn() — mock a callback function
2. jest.mock() — mock an entire module (e.g., an API client)
3. jest.spyOn() — spy on a method without changing its behavior
4. Timer mocks — jest.useFakeTimers()
5. Manual mocks — __mocks__ directory

Use a claims service that calls an external API as the example.

topics.title: Testing React Components
topics.description: Test your UI components with React Testing Library — test behavior, not implementation.
topics.claudePrompt: Write tests for a React LoginForm component using React Testing Library. The component has: email input, password input, submit button, error message display, and calls an onSubmit prop. Test: rendering, user typing, form submission, validation errors, loading state, and accessibility.
steps: Install: npm install --save-dev @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom
Write a test that renders your component and checks it appears
Write a test that simulates user interaction (type, click)
Write a test that checks error states
Ask Claude to review and suggest missing test scenarios

topics.title: Code Coverage & Quality Gates
topics.description: Set up Jest coverage reporting and establish minimum coverage thresholds.
steps: Run: npx jest --coverage
Open coverage/lcov-report/index.html in your browser
Find the files with lowest branch coverage
Ask Claude: 'Analyze this coverage report and tell me the riskiest uncovered code paths'
Add coverage thresholds to jest.config.js: coverageThreshold: { global: { branches: 70, functions: 80 } }

phases.checkpoint: You can write unit tests with Jest, mock dependencies, test React components, and generate coverage reports.

phases.title: Phase 3: Playwright for UI & API Testing
phases.description: The main event. Playwright handles both UI automation and API testing in one framework — the industry standard for modern E2E testing.

topics.title: Playwright Setup & First E2E Test
topics.description: Install Playwright, write a test that opens a browser and verifies a page.
steps: Run: npm init playwright@latest (choose TypeScript)
Explore the generated files: playwright.config.ts, tests/example.spec.ts
Run: npx playwright test — watch it execute
Run: npx playwright test --ui — see the visual test runner
Run: npx playwright show-report — see the HTML report
Modify the example test to visit your own app and verify something

topics.title: Selectors & Locators
topics.description: getByRole, getByTestId, getByText, getByLabel — how to find elements reliably. Why data-testid beats CSS selectors.
topics.claudePrompt:
Show me all Playwright locator strategies ranked by resilience:
1. getByRole (best)
2. getByLabel
3. getByText
4. getByTestId
5. CSS selectors (fragile)
6. XPath (worst)

For each, show an example and explain when to use it. Then show me how to refactor these fragile selectors into resilient ones:
- page.click('#btn-submit')
- page.click('.nav > ul > li:nth-child(3) > a')
- page.click('//div[@class="form"]/button[1]')

topics.title: UI Test Automation with Claude
topics.description: Use Claude to generate Playwright E2E tests from user stories.
topics.claudePrompt:
Generate a Playwright E2E test for this user story:

As a user, I want to log in to the application so that I can access my dashboard.

Acceptance criteria:
- Enter valid email and password → redirected to /dashboard
- Enter wrong password → see error message 'Invalid credentials'
- Leave email empty → see validation 'Email is required'
- Click 'Forgot Password' → navigated to /forgot-password

Use TypeScript, getByRole/getByTestId locators, and follow Playwright best practices. Include setup, assertions, and cleanup.
steps: Write a user story for a feature in your app
Ask Claude to generate the Playwright test
Run the test against your actual app
Fix any selector mismatches (real app vs generated test)
Add the test to your CI pipeline

topics.title: API Testing with Playwright
topics.description: Use Playwright's APIRequestContext for REST API testing — no browser needed, shared auth with UI tests.
topics.claudePrompt:
Write Playwright API tests for a CRUD REST API with these endpoints:
- GET /api/claims — list all claims
- GET /api/claims/:id — get one claim
- POST /api/claims — create a claim (body: claimType, employeeId, amount)
- PUT /api/claims/:id — update a claim
- DELETE /api/claims/:id — delete a claim

Include: happy paths, error handling (400, 404, 401), data validation, response time checks, and a full CRUD lifecycle test. Use TypeScript and the request fixture.
steps: Create tests/api/ directory for API tests
Configure baseURL in playwright.config.ts
Write a GET test that validates response structure
Write a POST test that creates and verifies a resource
Write a full CRUD lifecycle (create → read → update → delete → verify)
Write negative tests (invalid data, missing auth, not found)

topics.title: Mixing API + UI Tests
topics.description: Playwright's killer feature: create data via API (fast), verify in the browser (user-facing). Or vice versa.
topics.claudePrompt:
Write a Playwright test that:
1. Creates a claim via API (POST /api/claims) — this is fast setup
2. Navigates to the claims list page in the browser
3. Verifies the new claim appears in the UI
4. Clicks into the claim detail page
5. Verifies all fields match what the API returned
6. Deletes the claim via API (cleanup)

This demonstrates the API+UI testing pattern.
steps: Write a test that seeds data via API, then verifies it in the UI
Write a test that does an action in the UI, then verifies via API
Notice how much faster API setup is vs clicking through the UI
Refactor your slowest E2E tests to use API for setup/teardown

topics.title: Authentication & State Management
topics.description: Login once, reuse auth state across tests. Save minutes per test run.
topics.claudePrompt:
Show me how to set up Playwright's storageState to login once and reuse the session across all tests. Include:
1. Global setup file that logs in and saves state
2. playwright.config.ts that uses the saved state
3. A test that runs already-authenticated
4. How to handle multiple users (admin vs regular user)

topics.title: Page Object Model
topics.description: Organize your tests with the Page Object pattern — reusable, maintainable, and clean.
topics.claudePrompt:
Create a Page Object Model for a claims management app with these pages:
1. LoginPage — email, password, submit, error message
2. ClaimsListPage — search, filter, claim cards, create button
3. ClaimDetailPage — status, amount, dates, approve/deny buttons

Use TypeScript, Playwright locators, and include helper methods for common actions. Show me how a test uses these page objects.
steps: Create a pages/ directory
Extract selectors and actions from your tests into page classes
Refactor 3 existing tests to use page objects
Notice how much cleaner the tests read now

phases.checkpoint: You can write E2E tests, API tests, and mixed API+UI tests with Playwright. You use Page Objects and shared auth.

phases.title: Phase 4: AI-Powered Testing with Claude
phases.description: Level up your testing with AI. Use Claude for test generation, self-healing, security testing, data generation, and intelligent triage.

topics.title: AI Test Generation from Code
topics.description: Paste source code into Claude and get comprehensive test suites back in seconds.
topics.claudePrompt:
Write comprehensive tests for this code:

[PASTE CODE]

Requirements:
- Cover all branches, edge cases, and error paths
- Use Jest for unit tests or Playwright for API/E2E
- Include boundary values and negative scenarios
- Name tests as specifications (should_X_when_Y)
- Mock external dependencies
steps: Pick 3 functions from your codebase with no tests
Ask Claude to generate tests for each
Run them — fix any issues
Compare the coverage before vs after
Document patterns you learned from Claude's test design

topics.title: AI Test Generation from Requirements
topics.description: Give Claude a user story or spec document and get test cases + automation code.
topics.claudePrompt:
Generate test cases AND Playwright automation for this user story:

[PASTE USER STORY]

Provide:
1. Test case matrix (ID, scenario, steps, expected, priority)
2. Happy path Playwright test
3. Negative test cases
4. Edge cases
5. Accessibility tests
steps: Take a user story from your current sprint
Ask Claude for the test plan before writing any code
Generate the Playwright tests
Review: did Claude think of scenarios you wouldn't have?
Run the tests against your actual feature

topics.title: Self-Healing Tests with Claude
topics.description: When UI changes break your tests, use Claude to analyze failures and fix selectors automatically.
topics.claudePrompt:
My Playwright test is failing after a UI update. Fix the test:

## Failure Log:
[PASTE ERROR]

## Current Test Code:
[PASTE TEST]

## Current Page HTML:
[PASTE HTML]

Instructions:
1. Identify broken selectors
2. Find correct new selectors in the HTML
3. Use resilient selectors (data-testid > role > CSS)
4. Explain each change
5. Flag if test LOGIC needs changing, not just selectors
steps: Find a broken test in your suite (or intentionally break one)
Collect: error log, test code, and current HTML
Ask Claude to heal it
Review the fix — did it use resilient selectors?
Apply the pattern to prevent future breakage

topics.title: Synthetic Test Data Generation
topics.description: Use Claude to generate realistic, compliant test data from your database schemas.
topics.claudePrompt:
Generate test data for our database:

[PASTE SCHEMA]

Generate 20 records including:
- 10 happy path records (realistic, varied)
- 5 boundary value records (min/max of every field)
- 3 edge cases (null fields, max length strings, special characters)
- 2 security payloads (SQL injection, XSS)

Output as [SQL INSERT / JSON / CSV].
Mark each record with its test scenario.
steps: Export your database schema or API model
Ask Claude for test data targeting specific scenarios
Import the data into your test database
Write tests that use this data
Generate different datasets for different test environments

topics.title: AI Security Testing
topics.description: Use Claude to review your code for OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities and generate security-focused tests.
topics.claudePrompt:
Perform a security review of this API endpoint. Focus on OWASP Top 10:

[PASTE CODE]

For each finding:
1. Vulnerability type
2. Severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low)
3. Proof of concept exploit
4. Exact code fix
5. Playwright test that verifies the fix
steps: Pick your most exposed endpoint (login, file upload, search)
Ask Claude for a security review
Prioritize the findings by severity
Fix the top 3 issues
Write Playwright tests that verify the fixes
Add these security tests to your CI pipeline

topics.title: AI-Powered Test Triage
topics.description: When CI fails with many errors, use Claude to cluster failures by root cause and identify flaky tests.
topics.claudePrompt:
Our CI build failed with these test failures. Triage them:

[PASTE CI OUTPUT]

Provide:
1. Root cause clusters (group related failures)
2. Likely flaky tests (timing, race conditions)
3. Real bugs (actual code defects)
4. Priority fix order
5. One-line summary for the team Slack
steps: Save your next CI failure output
Ask Claude to triage it
Compare Claude's analysis vs your manual triage — how much time did it save?
Set up a team workflow: CI fails → paste to Claude → get triage → fix in order

phases.checkpoint: You can use Claude to generate tests, heal broken selectors, create test data, find security issues, and triage failures.

phases.title: Phase 5: CI/CD & Production Quality
phases.description: Put it all together. Automated tests in CI/CD, quality gates, monitoring, and a sustainable testing strategy.

topics.title: GitHub Actions for Test Automation
topics.description: Run your tests automatically on every push and pull request.
topics.claudePrompt:
Create a GitHub Actions workflow that:
1. Runs Jest unit tests
2. Runs Playwright API tests
3. Runs Playwright E2E tests (with browser install)
4. Generates and uploads coverage reports
5. Fails the PR if coverage drops below 70%
6. Uploads Playwright trace on failure for debugging

Use caching for node_modules and Playwright browsers.
steps: Create .github/workflows/tests.yml
Start with just unit tests on push
Add Playwright API tests
Add Playwright E2E tests with browser install
Add coverage thresholds as quality gates
Test by pushing a PR with a failing test

topics.title: Quality Gates & Branch Protection
topics.description: Enforce quality standards — no PR merges without passing tests and minimum coverage.
steps: Set up branch protection rules on main (require status checks)
Configure required checks: unit tests, API tests, E2E tests
Add coverage threshold: fail if coverage drops
Add lint checks (ESLint, TypeScript strict mode)
Test it: try merging a PR with failing tests — it should block

topics.title: Visual Regression Testing
topics.description: Catch UI bugs that functional tests miss by comparing screenshots.
topics.claudePrompt:
Show me how to set up Playwright visual regression testing:
1. Take baseline screenshots with toHaveScreenshot()
2. Compare on subsequent runs
3. Handle expected visual changes (update baselines)
4. Configure threshold for acceptable pixel differences
5. Integrate with CI/CD (store screenshots as artifacts)

Include handling for: responsive breakpoints, dark mode, and different browsers.

topics.title: Test Reporting & Dashboards
topics.description: Generate professional test reports the whole team can read.
steps: Configure Playwright HTML reporter (built-in)
Run: npx playwright show-report after test execution
Set up Allure reporting for richer dashboards (optional)
Add test reports as CI artifacts (download from GitHub Actions)
Share weekly test health summaries with the team

topics.title: Performance Testing Basics
topics.description: Monitor API response times in your tests. Catch performance regressions before production.
topics.claudePrompt:
Show me how to add performance assertions to Playwright tests:
1. Measure API response times with Date.now()
2. Assert response time < threshold
3. Measure page load times with Performance API
4. Track Core Web Vitals (LCP, FID, CLS) in Playwright
5. Create a simple performance regression test suite

topics.title: Building a Testing Strategy
topics.description: Put it all together — create a testing strategy document for your team.
topics.claudePrompt:
Help me create a testing strategy document for our team. Our tech stack is:
- Frontend: React + TypeScript
- Backend: Node.js / Open Liberty (Java)
- Database: PostgreSQL
- CI/CD: GitHub Actions
- Testing tools: Jest, Playwright, Claude

Include:
1. Testing pyramid breakdown (how many of each type)
2. What to test at each level
3. Coverage targets by module type
4. AI-assisted testing workflow
5. CI/CD pipeline design
6. Quality gates
7. Team responsibilities
8. When to write tests (TDD vs test-after)
steps: Audit your current test coverage and testing gaps
Ask Claude to generate a testing strategy template
Customize it for your team's stack and workflow
Present it at a team meeting
Iterate based on feedback and real-world results

phases.checkpoint: You have automated tests in CI/CD with quality gates, visual regression, performance monitoring, and a documented testing strategy.

tools.name: Jest
tools.description: JavaScript testing framework for unit & integration tests

tools.name: Playwright
tools.description: E2E testing, API testing, and visual regression in one framework

tools.name: React Testing Library
tools.description: Test React components by behavior, not implementation

tools.name: Claude
tools.description: AI co-pilot for test generation, code review, and triage

tools.name: GitHub Actions
tools.description: CI/CD pipeline for automated test execution

tools.name: VS Code Playwright Extension
tools.description: Run, debug, and record Playwright tests in VS Code

books.title: The Art of Unit Testing
books.author: Roy Osherove
books.description: The definitive guide to writing maintainable, readable, and trustworthy unit tests.

books.title: Testing JavaScript Applications
books.author: Lucas da Costa
books.description: From unit tests to E2E, focused on the JavaScript ecosystem.

books.title: Continuous Testing for DevOps Professionals
books.author: Eran Kinsbruner
books.description: Testing in CI/CD pipelines, shift-left, and AI in testing.
